ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific 9-piece serving code; A0EJX (sweet fried dough pastry) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for churros; none assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 9 churro sticks, approximately 135 g total, as sold by Churros Republic at Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18617)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy data for plain churros (deep-fried wheat dough) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent plain churros without chocolate dipping sauce. Serving size estimated at 135 g for 9 standard-sized churro sticks (approximately 15 g each), consistent with typical commercial portions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros specifically. GI of 74 is estimated from deep-fried wheat dough pastries (analogous to doughnuts and fried wheat-flour snacks) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (University of Sydney GI Database). Doughnuts (plain, deep-fried wheat flour) have a reported GI of approximately 74 (glucose reference). Churros share the same base composition: refined wheat flour, deep-fried, with added sugar coating. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (74 x 64.8) / 100 = 47.95, rounded to 48.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 48.0 g/100g x 135 g serving = 64.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content and high refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit churros consumption to occasional small portions, as the sodium content of approximately 290 mg per 100 g and the high refined carbohydrate load can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 74) and high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit churros, as the high GI of approximately 74 and a glycemic load of approximately 48 per 9-piece serving will cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise, making glycaemic management significantly more difficult.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat from deep-frying and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume churros only occasionally, as the high total fat content (approximately 18 g per 100 g) from deep-frying in vegetable oil, combined with refined carbohydrates and added sugar, contributes to an unfavourable lipid and glycaemic profile when eaten frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros are a traditional Spanish fried pastry made from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, and salt, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried in hot oil until golden and crispy on the outside with a soft, chewy interior. They are typically coated in cinnamon sugar immediately after frying, giving them a sweet, aromatic finish. In Singapore, churros are sold as a popular dessert and snack item at specialty cafes such as Churros Republic, where they are served in portions of 9 pieces. The 'Krispy' variant emphasises an extra-crunchy exterior achieved through precise frying technique. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense at approximately 375 kcal per 100 g, with a significant carbohydrate content of around 48 g per 100 g, predominantly refined starch and added sugar. The fat content of approximately 18 g per 100 g reflects the deep-frying process. The glycemic index is estimated at 74 (high), making churros a high-glycaemic food that causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation. They are not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. Churros are generally vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly in their plain form, though dipping sauces may alter allergen and dietary status.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of churros?

confidence_reason: No Singapore-specific or HPB nutrient data exists for churros. USDA SR Legacy data for plain deep-fried churros is used as the closest approved primary source. GI is estimated from analogous deep-fried wheat dough pastries (doughnuts) per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Serving size of 135 g for 9 pieces is estimated from typical commercial churro stick weights. Confidence is medium due to the adapted nutrient source and analogous GI estimation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for plain churros (deep-fried wheat dough). No HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for churros. 2. Serving size of 135 g (9 pieces at approximately 15 g each) is estimated from typical commercial churro dimensions; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 3. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried plain doughnuts from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct churros GI measurement was found in approved sources. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA data for plain churros made without eggs or dairy; this is a confirmed zero value. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as churros contain no vitamin C-bearing ingredients. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported; churros contain no animal-derived ingredients in plain form. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this item. 13. The food_name 'Krispy 9 pcs' from the input is a menu-style commercial label; canonical English name 'Churros (9 pieces)' is used per Veritas naming rules. 14. Cuisine is classified as Spanish per input and ontology alignment. Multilingual names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this food is sold in Singapore context. 15. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302649 for 'churro' is used as exact match. FoodEx2 code A0EJX is used as closest parent for sweet fried dough pastry.
